extraction.one_line_summary: RODRIGUEZ Oscar soloed from breakaway to win atop 3km/14% wall; QUINTANA dropped YATES in final 50m for 6-second gain.
tactical_patterns: Movistar controlled pace despite not holding leader's jersey, protecting two top-5 riders like race favorites, revealing confidence hierarchy.
32-rider breakaway allowed freedom on first of three summit finishes, suggesting GC teams conserving for Sunday's historic Covadonga finish.
Steep 14% gradient negated traditional attacks; riders forced to maintain threshold rather than explosive accelerations, favoring steady climbers.
Quintana delayed sprint until final 50m expecting Yates counter-punch; Yates poker-faced weakness until too late to respond.

rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: positive: Strongest on terrain matching previous win, chipped 6 seconds despite full team effort suggesting deeper form reserves remain
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: Concealed weakness effectively but yielded time on signature steep finish, possibly deliberately conserving for Sunday
rider_signals.MAJKA Rafal: negative: First to attack breakaway but dropped by Rodriguez with 1.5km remaining, suggesting insufficient form for pure steep gradient
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Oscar: positive: Bridged to leaders then immediately dropped them on 14% gradient, revealing exceptional steep-climbing capacity under radar
